title: Methods
text:
The validation dataset is GSE60450, a mouse mammary gland RNA-seq study comparing luminal and basal cell populations across three developmental stages (virgin, 18.5 day pregnancy, 2 day lactation). Raw count data were downloaded from the GEO FTP server.
Citation: Fu NY et al. EGF-mediated induction of Mcl-1 at the switch to lactation is essential for alveolar cell survival. Nature Cell Biology 17, 365–375 (2015). https://doi.org/10.1038/ncb3117

title: Methods
text:
The validation dataset is GSE60450, a mouse mammary gland RNA-seq study comparing luminal and basal cell populations across three developmental stages (virgin, 18.5 day pregnancy, 2 day lactation). Raw count data were downloaded from the GEO FTP server.
Citation: Fu NY et al. EGF-mediated induction of Mcl-1 at the switch to lactation is essential for alveolar cell survival. Nature Cell Biology 17, 365–375 (2015). https://doi.org/10.1038/ncb3117

title: Methods
text:
Differential expression

R implementation
Counts were normalised using TMM normalisation (edgeR) and transformed with voom precision weights (limma). Differential expression between basal and luminal cells was assessed using a linear model with empirical Bayes moderation (limma::eBayes()). P-values were adjusted using the Benjamini–Hochberg method.


Python implementation
Counts were normalised to log2-CPM and differential expression was assessed using a two-sample t-test (scipy) with Benjamini–Hochberg correction (statsmodels). This is an approximation of the R limma-voom method and is provided to demonstrate the Python framework — for publishable results use the R implementation.

title: Methods
text:
Gene Ontology enrichment

R implementation
GO enrichment was performed using clusterProfiler with the mouse annotation database org.Mm.eg.db (offline). All three ontologies (BP, MF, CC) are tested.


Python implementation
GO enrichment was performed using gseapy via the Enrichr web API. Internet access is required from the server running the app.

title: Methods
text:
Reproducibility
Both apps are designed following the ten rules described in the manuscript. All dependencies are locked:

R: renv.lock
Python: shiny_app_py_env.yml + requirements.txt

To exactly reproduce the environment:
# R
Rscript -e "renv::restore()"

# Python
conda env create -f Python/shiny_app_py_env.yml
pip install -r Python/requirements.txt

title: Limma DEG Interactive Explorer
text:
Interactive web applications for differential gene expression analysis
    using limma-voom, implemented in both R and Python Shiny.

title: Limma DEG Interactive Explorer
text:
About
This resource accompanies the manuscript:

[PAPER TITLE]
Patricia Agudelo-Romero · The Kids Research Institute Australia
[Journal Name], [Year]. DOI: [DOI]

The paper describes ten practical rules for building reproducible, interactive data analysis applications in the life sciences. Two parallel implementations of the same Limma DEG Explorer app — one in R Shiny and one in Python Shiny — serve as the worked example throughout the manuscript.
Both applications accept a CSV file produced by limma::topTable() and provide real-time interactive visualisation of:

Differentially expressed genes (volcano plot, ranked bar plot, DEG table)
Gene Ontology enrichment (dot plot + table)
Downloadable results folder (HTML report, CSV, Excel)

The validation dataset is GSE60450 (mouse mammary gland luminal vs basal cells; Fu et al. 2015).

title: Limma DEG Interactive Explorer
text:
Launch the apps


  
  
    📊
    R Shiny
    
      Built with R Shiny, ggplot2, limma-voom, clusterProfiler,
      and org.Mm.eg.db. Runs fully offline.
    
    
      Open R App →
    
  

  
  
    🐍
    Python Shiny
    
      Built with Python Shiny, matplotlib, scipy, gseapy (Enrichr API),
      and Jinja2. Requires internet for GO enrichment.
    
    
      Open Python App →

title: Limma DEG Interactive Explorer
text:
Reproduce the analysis
All code is available on GitHub. To run either app locally:
R:
# Install dependencies
source("R/00_setup.R")

# Download and prepare data
source("R/01_data_prep.R")

# Launch app
shiny::runApp("R/")
Python:
# Create environment and install dependencies
python Python/00_setup.py

# Activate environment
conda activate shiny_app_py

# Download and prepare data
python Python/01_data_prep.py

# Launch app
shiny run Python/app.py
